reasoning: The base probability of any single country winning Eurovision is roughly ~2.5% (1 in ~37 participants). Germany has had a historically poor track record in recent years, often placing at or near the bottom in the grand final. On February 20, the Polymarket price for a German victory was already extremely low at 0.02 (2%). On March 1, Germany officially selected Abor & Tynna with the song "Baller" as their representative. The market reaction was sharply negative, causing Germany to plummet 8 places down to become the 21st favorite to win in overall betting odds. In Eurovision betting markets, the top 5 or 10 countries heavily dominate the implied probability distribution (usually ~80-90% combined), meaning the 21st favorite has an implied probability of less than 1%. Due to the parameter bounds of this forecasting system, I am forecasting at the lowest permitted probability limit of 0.02 (2%), though my true estimate is <1%.
